document_identity.parent_chain: UPL → SWSH → SSH → SUSH → Subway Funding Holdco → Subway Funding LLC → DAL
document_identity.issuance_date: April 25, 2025, amended September 4, 2025, and amended December 1, 2025

item1_franchisor.formed: Converted from Florida LLC on October 29, 2018; converted to Delaware LLC on May 29, 2024
item1_franchisor.franchise_history: Franchising Subway restaurants since 1965. Previously offered plush toy franchises 1982-1983 (none sold).
item1_franchisor.acquisition: Acquired by Roark Capital via Underground Purchaser LLC on April 30, 2024
item1_franchisor.securitization: US Securitization Transaction closed June 20, 2024. Series 2024-1 ($3.35B) and 2024-3 ($2.335B) fixed-rate senior secured notes. $93M variable-rate term notes. $400M revolving facility.
item1_franchisor.ip_holder: Subway US IP Holder LLC — all US trademarks transferred; 99-year royalty-free license to DAL
item1_franchisor.management: Franchise World Headquarters LLC (FWH) manages operations as securitization manager
item1_franchisor.international: Master Franchise Businesses sold in 30+ countries via SIP and SIBV
item1_franchisor.business_developers: Independent contractors who assist with franchise sales; receive ~half franchise fee + up to 1/3 royalties
item1_franchisor.affiliated_programs: Roark Capital portfolio includes other QSR and franchise brands

item2_leadership.name: Various officers and directors listed
item2_leadership.role: Officers/Directors of DAL, SWSH, FWH, FWHT, and affiliates

item2_leadership.name: David Strawhince
item2_leadership.role: Senior Global Account Manager of FWH

item2_leadership.name: Faneva Durandisse
item2_leadership.role: Assistant Manager of Non-Traditional of FWH

item4_bankruptcy.details: Benjamin Selden (CFO of SWSH, SUIPH, FWH, FWHT, DAL, Subway MyWay LLC) was SVP Finance at Sungard Availability Services which filed Chapter 11 on May 1, 2019. Plan confirmed May 2, 2019; cases closed September 6, 2019.

item5_initial_fees.refund_policy: Non-refundable except: (1) location not approved within 90 days, (2) dual location test no sublease — half fee refunded in either case
item5_initial_fees.development_fee: Current franchise fee × number of restaurants

item6_royalty.reduced_rates: 6.5%-8% for airports/train stations/captive travel plazas; 7.5%-8% for certain DA holders
item6_royalty.gross_sales_definition: All sales of every kind made from restaurant; excludes state/local sales taxes

item6_advertising.reduced_rates: 0.5%-2% for some non-traditional/satellite; 2%-3.5% for certain DA holders
item6_advertising.legacy_provision: Franchise agreements with 2/3 vote right to increase advertising (>2/3 of restaurants under legacy agreements); current agreements fix at 4.5%

item6_technology_fees.sub_club: 1.9% of gross sales per loyalty transaction

item6_rent.sublease_structure: Franchisor affiliate holds master lease; franchisee signs sublease. Affiliate may profit from spread.
item6_rent.direct_lease_option: Available with franchisor approval; must execute Franchisor Lease Rider

item8_supplier_restrictions.approval_criteria: Proprietary trade secret — not disclosed to franchisees
item8_supplier_restrictions.supplier_approval_timeline: Two weeks to six months generally

item9_obligations.summary: Comprehensive obligation table cross-referencing FA, FAR, DLTR, CBLR, MUFA, and DA to FDD items. Key obligations: site selection, pre-opening purchases, fees, compliance, trademarks, product restrictions.

item11_training.ongoing_training: University of Subway online; remedial training if standards not met

item12_territory.franchisor_competition_rights: Unlimited — franchisor and affiliates retain right to compete in any form
item12_territory.roark_portfolio_competition: Roark Capital portfolio brands may compete in same areas
item12_territory.alternative_channels: Centralized call center, internet, catalog, telemarketing, direct marketing — no compensation to franchisee
item12_territory.relocation_rights: Permitted if lease terminates without fault; at franchisee's sole expense; 6 months for satellite

item14_patents.copyrights: Various works claimed; not all registered
item14_patents.operations_manual: Proprietary trade secret; licensed from SUIPH

item15_participation.noncompete_during_term: No association with competitive business anywhere
item15_participation.noncompete_post_term: 1 year, 3-mile radius from any Subway that operates or operated in prior year

item16_product_restrictions.mop_program: Marketwide Option Program — decisions by market vote
item16_product_restrictions.sop_program: Store Option Program — decisions by individual franchisee
item16_product_restrictions.co_branding: Requires separate franchise agreement with third-party franchisor

item17_renewal.renewal_fee: $3,750 (25% of current franchise fee); $1,250 satellite; $1,000 short-term satellite

item17_termination.franchisee_termination_right: None (may seek arbitration if franchisor defaults within 60 days)
non_curable_defaults: Repeated failures (3 in 12 months or 2 same in 6 months)

item17_noncompete.during_term: No direct or indirect association with competitive business anywhere
item17_noncompete.post_term_radius: 3 miles from any Subway that operates or operated within the prior year
item17_noncompete.violation_penalty: $15,000 per competing business + 8% of its gross sales

item17_dispute_resolution.method: Mandatory arbitration (AAA or ADRC, at filing party's discretion)
item17_dispute_resolution.damages_limitations: No incidental, exemplary, contingent, punitive, or consequential damages (except where prohibited by law)

item19_fpr.no_fpr_statement: We do not make any representations about a franchisee's future financial performance or the past financial performance of company-owned or franchised outlets.

item20_outlet_summary.gag_clause_quote: During the last three fiscal years, we have signed confidentiality clauses with current or former franchisees which restrict them from speaking openly with you about their experience with us.
item20_outlet_summary.independent_franchisee_org: North American Association of Subway Franchisees (NAASF), Fairfield, CT; (203) 579-7779

key_notes.securitization_guarantee: $5.685B in securitized notes guaranteed by DAL's assets
key_notes.restricted_cash_purpose: Securitization note reserves (interest, principal, commitment fee)
key_notes.disregarded_entity: Single-member LLC; taxed at parent level
key_notes.no_impairment: No indications of impairment identified for 2024 and 2023

item22_contracts_listed: Exhibit A-10 — Dual Location Test Rider
Exhibit D-6 — Franchisee Acceptance of Renegotiation
Exhibit D-7 — Lease and Sublease Termination Agreement
Exhibit G-4 — Amendment to FA (Additional Term)
Exhibit K-1 — DAL Promissory Note and Security Agreement
Exhibit K-2 — Huntington Technology Finance Equipment Lease
Exhibit M — Subway Global Privacy Statement

special_risks.description: Franchise agreement requires disputes resolved by arbitration and/or litigation only in Connecticut. May force less favorable settlement and higher costs.

unresolveds.description: Unaudited interim financial statements (Oct 31, 2025) — referenced in Item 21 but exact page location in extended Exhibit C needs confirmation
Business Developer compensation formulas and exact impact on franchisor economics — approximated but not fully transparent
Securitization implications for franchisee claims in potential DAL default — $5.685B in notes guaranteed by DAL assets; franchisee position in priority unclear
Fresh Forward 2.0 remodel costs for non-traditional and drive-thru formats — 'cannot be estimated at this time'
Future Digital Technology Fee — reserved right to impose but amount undisclosed

contradictions.description: Operating expenses dropped from $941.8M (2023) to $567.4M (2024) while revenue stayed flat ($971.9M vs $975.3M) — driven by restructured affiliate fee arrangements post-securitization, not organic improvement. Net income jumped from $15.4M to $397.0M. Buyers should not interpret 2024 financials as representative of normalized franchisor economics.
